summary.narrative: Governor Kay Ivey, Alabama's 54th governor, campaigned on traditional conservative themes emphasizing job creation, education improvement, and fiscal responsibility. Her administration pursued rural economic development, broadband expansion, and education initiatives including third-grade reading proficiency goals and school choice expansion. Ivey has prioritized healthcare access through Medicaid care-management programs while opposing Medicaid expansion. Her tenure saw documented implementation of several infrastructure and economic development commitments.
summary.grade_rationale: Ivey demonstrated concrete follow-through on broadband access, rural economic investment, and education initiatives, with measurable outcomes (broadband ranking improvement, $1.7B rural investment). However, vague framing of several commitments and limited documentation on third-grade reading initiative outcomes prevent higher grades. Healthcare expansion promise remained unfulfilled by design (opposed Medicaid expansion).

promises.normalized: Improve broadband access across Alabama to top 25 nationally
Achieve $1.7B+ rural investment and 1,700+ jobs annually
Get every Alabama student reading at grade level by 3rd grade
Expand school choice through education tax credits
Protect IVF access through legal liability protections
Enhance Medicaid long-term care management system

data_gaps: Measurable outcomes for third-grade reading initiative not documented
2nd Amendment policy specifics and implementation details absent
Details on 'good paying jobs' commitment metrics or targets unclear
School choice program enrollment numbers and utilization rates not provided
Specific timeline and completion status for education infrastructure projects undefined
Rural broadband access metrics by county not granularly available
Implementation status of AI election integrity bill not assessed
